title: HPCET Application Form 2026 - Overview

detail.title: HPCET Application Form 2026 - Overview
detail.short_info: HPCET Application Form 2026 is now available at Himachal Pradesh Technical University (HPTU) from 13 March to 19 April 2026. Check eligibility, important dates, fees, exam pattern and step-by-step online application process here.

content.text: HPCET Application Form 2026 is now open on the official portal of Himachal Pradesh Technical University (HPTU), Hamirpur. The online application window opened on 13 March 2026 and will remain active until 19 April 2026. HPCET 2026 (Himachal Pradesh Common Entrance Test) is conducted for admissions to B.Tech, B.Pharmacy, MCA, MBA, MBA (T&HM), B.Sc. (HM&CT)/BHMCT, M.Sc. Physics and M.Sc. Environmental Sciences courses offered by HPTU and its affiliated colleges for academic session 2026-27.

Candidates who have passed 10+2 or a graduation degree (depending on course) from a recognized board/university are eligible to apply. This page covers the direct HPCET 2026 application link, complete eligibility criteria, exam dates, application fee, step-by-step guide to apply online, and all necessary information to help you fill the form without any errors.
Download Official Notification PDF
Apply Online - himtu.ac.in

The HPCET 2026 application form is available only in online mode via HPTU's official website. Candidates must register via the Him Access Portal and complete all four profile tabs before applying for courses. The exam will be conducted in offline (OMR-based) mode. Official Website for Applying: www.himtu.ac.in

rows: B.Tech, B.Pharmacy, MCA, MBA, MBA (T&HM), B.Sc. (HM&CT)/BHMCT, M.Sc. Physics, M.Sc. Environmental Sciences

content.text: Candidates must keep track of all HPCET 2026 important dates to avoid missing any deadline. The University may update dates — keep visiting www.himtu.ac.in for latest updates.

rows: Last Date to Submit Application Form
Centralized Counselling for UG & PG Courses
Last week of July / 1st week of August 2026

content.text: The minimum eligibility criteria for HPCET 2026 are as per AICTE/PCI/UGC norms. Candidates appearing in their final 10+2 or graduation examination in March/April 2026 are also eligible to apply, subject to fulfilling eligibility at the time of admission. Check course-wise eligibility below:

rows: 10+2 with Physics / Mathematics / Chemistry / Computer Science / Electronics / IT / Biology / Informatics Practices / Biotechnology / Technical Vocational / Agriculture / Engineering Graphics / Business Studies / Entrepreneurship. Min. 45% marks (40% for reserved). OR Passed D.Voc. Stream in same/allied sector.
10+2 with Physics & Chemistry (compulsory) + one of Mathematics/Biology. Min. 45% marks (40% reserved). Age: 17 years on or before 31 December of admission year.
10+2 in any stream from a recognized Board. Min. 45% marks (40% reserved) in qualifying exam.
Bachelor's degree of minimum 3 years duration. Min. 50% marks (45% reserved).
Any graduation degree (B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc/B.Com/B.A/B.Voc/BCA etc.), preferably with Mathematics at 10+2 or graduation level. Min. 50% marks (45% reserved). Bridge course for non-Maths candidates.
B.Sc. with Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics OR Honors in Physics. Min. 50% marks in aggregate over 3 years (45% reserved).
Any Bachelor/UG degree in basic/applied sciences including Engineering/Medical Sciences/Pharmacy/Architecture. Min. 50% marks (45% reserved).

sections.heading: HPCET 2026 - Application Fee/ Entrance Test Fee

content.text: The application fee must be paid online during form submission. Fee once paid is non-refundable. In case of multiple payments against the same form, a refund can be requested via the fee refund form available on HPTU website (Examination → Download Forms → Examination/Reevaluation fee refund form); processing may take about one month.

sections.heading: How to Apply for HPCET 2026 - Step-by-Step Guide

content.text: Applications are accepted only in online mode via the HPTU official website. Follow the steps below carefully to fill the HPCET 2026 application form without errors:

items: Visit the official HPTU website www.himtu.ac.in and navigate to the HPCET 2026 application page. Click on One Time Registration (OTR) under the "Services" section.
Click "Register →" and then "As Student". New users must click "New User? Sign up for Citizen Login".
You will be redirected to the Him Access Portal. Choose the Aadhaar number option, provide eKYC consent, enter OTP, then provide your mobile number and email to generate a unique Him Access ID.
Login "As Student" using your Him Access ID / registered mobile number or other available option.
Update your profile in four tabs: Applicant Details, Contact Details, Qualifications (10th, 12th, graduation if applicable), and Upload Photo & Signature (JPG/JPEG/PNG, 60-100 KB).
Click "Complete Profile" → Logout → Login again → navigate to "Courses List".
Under UG/PG list, click "Apply" against your desired course or combined course option (e.g., B.Tech & B.Pharmacy both, MBA & MCA, etc.).
Fill the HPCET 2026 Application Form: select quota (AIQ / HPSQ / NRI / KM), category, minimum qualification, sub-category etc. Ensure all entries match your profile/qualification details.

Tick the declaration checkbox, then click "Save Draft". Check under "My Applications", use "Edit Application" if corrections are needed, then click "Submit Application". After submission, no further edits are allowed.
Note down the Application Number (format: HPCET26XXXXXX) displayed on screen. Go to "My Applications" → click "₹ Pay XXXX" → Proceed to Payment and complete the fee payment via the payment gateway.

After successful payment, download and print the application form PDF. If the PDF icon does not appear, click the payment button again to refresh payment status. If not generated within 2 working days, email: [email protected] with your Form Number, transaction ID, and payment screenshot.

content.text: Keep the following documents ready in digital format before starting the HPCET 2026 application form. All documents must be scanned clearly and in the prescribed format/size.

items: Recent passport-size photograph (JPG/JPEG/PNG, 60-100 KB)
Candidate's signature in prescribed format (JPG/JPEG/PNG, 60-100 KB)
Aadhaar Card (for Him Access Portal registration)
Class 10 (Matric) mark sheet and certificate
Class 12 (10+2) mark sheet and certificate
Graduation degree mark sheet (for PG course applicants - MCA, MBA, M.Sc.)
Caste/Category certificate - SC/ST/OBC/EWS (if applicable)
BPL certificate (if claiming BPL fee concession)
Valid photo ID proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID/Passport)

content.text: HPCET 2026 is conducted in offline (OMR-based) mode in English language. The exam pattern varies by course. There is negative marking in all courses. Refer to the course-wise patterns below:
1. B.Tech & B.Pharmacy (Allopathy) - Direct Entry
Duration: 3 Hours 15 Minutes | Marking: +4 for correct, −1 for wrong, 0 for unattempted. In Section B, if more than 30 questions are attempted, only the first 30 are evaluated.
2. B.Sc. (HM&CT) / BHMCT
Duration: 2 Hours | Marking: +1 for correct, −0.25 for wrong, 0 for unattempted.
3. MBA / MBA (T&HM) / MCA
Duration: 2 Hours | Marking: +4 for correct, −1 for wrong, 0 for unattempted.
4. M.Sc. Physics

rows: Mathematical Methods, Classical Mechanics & General Properties of Matter
Atomic & Molecular Physics, KTG & Thermodynamics, Solid State & Electronics

content.text: Duration: 2 Hours | Marking: +1 for correct, −0.25 for wrong, 0 for unattempted.
5. M.Sc. Environmental Sciences
Duration: 2 Hours | Marking: +1 for correct, −0.25 for wrong, 0 for unattempted.

The HPCET 2026 Result is tentatively expected by 21 May 2026 on the official HPTU website. After result declaration, candidates will need to apply separately for centralized counselling using a new online form (Prospectus Part-II: Admission Brochure 2026-27) which will be released in May 2026.

Admission to all courses is made on the basis of merit (rank/marks) in HPCET 2026 or applicable National Level Entrance Tests, subject to fulfillment of minimum educational qualification. Centralized counselling is tentatively scheduled for June 2026 and classes are expected to start from the last week of July or first week of August 2026.

Exam centres for HPCET 2026 vary by course. The University may add, change, or cancel centres depending on the number of applicants. Candidates should select their preferred centre carefully during form submission; centre change requests (if approved) attract a fee of ₹2000.

rows: Bilaspur, Chamba, Hamirpur, Mandi, Kangra, Shimla, Solan, Kullu, Sirmour, Una, and Chandigarh
MCA, MBA, MBA (T&HM), B.Sc. (HM&CT)/BHMCT, M.Sc. Physics, M.Sc. Environmental Sciences
